title: Cirrus: Skip most tests on tag-push

comments.body: ***Note:*** [I'm forcing Cirrus to think this PR was from a tag-push](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8900/commits/ffafbdc982eb9d57d3f77837de52e94980eb7655).  This correctly results in [a *Verify Release* task failure](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5213822464032768?command=main#L6), as [is expected.](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/5278)

Here's the map, in case you find it useful. (It's what I'm primarily referring to for my review):
![cirrus-map-pr8900](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/103807907-5d4fa200-5014-11eb-86c5-3b1ed8d5e0e8.png)

There's one thing I find unintuitive: there are three `alt_build` tasks (`alt_build`, `osx_alt_build`, `static_alt_build`). Each of those has different SKIPs. I see no way to consolidate those (yes, I tried) but should they maybe share the same SKIPs?

> Each of those has different SKIPs

Oh...actually, I think all of the 'alt build' tasks should run for a tag-push...my bad.

Friendly suggestion:
```diff
diff --git a/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh b/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh
index 7f9afd1fd..baf6f31fe 100755
--- a/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh
+++ b/contrib/cirrus/runner.sh
@@ -199,8 +199,9 @@ function _run_altbuild() {
 }

 function _run_release() {
-    if bin/podman info |& grep -Eq -- '-dev'; then
-        die "Releases must never contain '-dev' in output of 'podman info'"
+    dev=$(bin/podman info |& grep -- -dev)
+    if [[ -n "$dev" ]]; then
+        die "Releases must never contain '-dev' in output of 'podman info' ($dev)"
     fi
 }

```

> Friendly suggestion:

@edsantiago this is also a valid approach, though I don't see a large benefit to justify changing the code at this time.  I'm assuming that before too long, all the release-processing/testing code will be both expanded and re-implemented.  This PR is just a stop-gap while the group solves the bigger release problems...in case a release needs to go out before improvements are in place.

>though I don't see a large benefit to justify changing the code at this time.

Okay, I'll change "friendly suggestion" to "kind of important and insistent recommendation".

Here is the workflow:
1. I looked at the test failure
2. It told me "blah blah -dev blah blah"
3. It did not give any useful information whatsoever, so I had no way of knowing what it was complaining about, or what the `-dev` string was

Someone trying to debug this problem might not have the exact same setup as CI. Even if they do, it takes time and energy to check that out and rebuild. Including the offending `-dev` strings in the error output can save someone time. Please have consideration for future debuggers.

> Please have consideration for future debuggers.

Oh your contention is about the message, not the checking itself...I'm more than willing to improve that aspect...

@edsantiago [improved failure output](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6151653336285184):

```
Checking podman release (or potential release) criteria.
************************************************
ERROR: Found unexpected '-dev' in output of 'podman info':
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.19.0-dev
...cut...
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.0.0-dev  (contrib/cirrus/runner.sh:211 in _run_release())
************************************************
```
Okay, tests are green.  I opened a Jira card WRT research and planning for an improved/better release process (and automation) RE: the two issues this PR will close when merged.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
